workflow.name: Optimize Webflow CMS SEO metadata using MCP and GPT‑4o-mini
workflow.description:
## Who's it for

Marketing teams, content creators, and agencies managing Webflow CMS sites who need to optimize SEO metadata at scale.

## How it works

The workflow fetches CMS items from Webflow, then uses AI to rewrite titles and descriptions to meet SEO best practices (50-60 character titles, 120-155 character meta descriptions). It automatically publishes changes back to Webflow and logs all updates to Google Sheets for audit purposes.

## Requirements

- Webflow site with CMS collections
- MCP Webflow OAuth2 credentials
- Any LLM api
- Google Sheets for logging (optional)

## How to set up

1. **Get your collection ID**: In Webflow, go to your CMS collection → copy the collection ID
2. **Update Set Fields node**: Replace collectionId with yours
3. **Update Format for Update node**: Replace collectionId
4. **Match field names**: If your collection uses different fields than "name" and "project-summary", update the agent prompt and Format for Update code
5. **Test first**: Set batch size to 1 and run on a single item
6. **Scale up**: Increase batch size gradually


## How to customize

**Change character limits**: Adjust minTitleLength, maxTitleLength, minSummaryLength, maxSummaryLength in Set Fields node. Add more fields like alt-text, post body, slug, etc.

**Different field names**: Update agent system prompt, Structured Output Parser schema, and Format for Update code

workflow.name: Optimize SEO metadata for Webflow CMS using MCP and AI

value.context: Retrieving collection items for SEO analysis

options.systemMessage:
=You are an SEO optimization specialist for Webflow CMS.

Your task: Optimize items to meet these requirements:
- name (title): {{ $('Set Fields').first().json.minTitleLength }}-{{ $('Set Fields').first().json.maxTitleLength }} characters
- project-summary: {{ $('Set Fields').first().json.minSummaryLength }}-{{ $('Set Fields').first().json.maxSummaryLength }} characters

For each item:
1. Optimize name if outside target range
2. Optimize project-summary if outside target range
3. Check services-rendered for H1/H2 structure
4. Flag missing alt text
5. Count words in services-rendered

Output: JSON array ONLY. NO explanations. NO markdown. Start with [ and end with ].

Structure:
[
  {
    "id": "string",
    "needsUpdate": true,
    "status": "optimized | attention_required",
    "updates": {
      "name": "string or null",
      "project-summary": "string or null"
    },
    "audit": {
      "charCount_name": 0,
      "charCount_summary": 0,
      "wordCount_services": 0
    },
    "issues": ["MISSING_ALT", "THIN_CONTENT", "INVALID_H_STRUCTURE"]
  }
]


Constraints:
- Never modify: id, slug, _archived, _draft, featured-project, color
- Keep brand voice professional
- Include target keywords naturally

parameters.content:
# 🚀 Webflow CMS SEO Bulk Optimizer
This workflow optimizes SEO metadata for Webflow CMS collections using AI.

**What it does:**
- Fetches CMS items from your Webflow collection
- Analyzes titles and descriptions against SEO best practices
- Optimizes length (titles: 50-60 chars, summaries: 120-155 chars)
- Checks for missing alt text and content structure
- Updates and publishes items back to Webflow
- Logs all changes to Google Sheets

**Requirements:**
- Webflow MCP OAuth2 credentials
- OpenRouter or any other LLM API key 
- Google Sheets or similar
- Your Webflow collection ID

**Before running:**
If your collection has different field names:
1. **Agent system prompt**: Replace "name" and "project-summary" with your field names
2. **Structured Output Parser schema**: Update field names in JSON schema
3. **Format for Update code**: Change field names 

**Common field names (Webflow default collections):**
- Posts: name, post-summary, post-body
- Products: name, description, short-description
- Projects: name, project-summary, services-rendered

## Webflow MCP Config

**Create credentials:**
- Select MCP OAuth2 API
- Set **Server URL** to https://mcp.webflow.com/mcp
- Click Save and authorize access to your Webflow workspaces and sites

**Configure MCP Client nodes:**
- Set MCP Endpoint URL to https://mcp.webflow.com/mcp
- Select **data_cms_tool** (for CMS operations). For other operations, select the appropriate tool.
- **Actions** and **Context** depend on the tool and your use case—see examples in the workflow nodes.

### MCP Webflow node errors i've encountered

**Authentication:**
- Credential type: MCP OAuth2 API
- Server URL: https://mcp.webflow.com/mcp
- Must authorize Webflow workspaces after saving

**Common errors:**

**404 - Resource not found**
→ Wrong collection ID. Get from Webflow CMS URL.

**400 - Bad Request**
→ Check JSON structure has "request" wrapper
→ Remove null values from fieldData
→ Verify parameter names (itemIds not itemIDs)

**400 - Invalid input for 'actions'**
→ Missing "request" object inside action
→ Wrong JSON structure

**Items not updating:**
→ Field names don't match collection schema
→ Use exact field names from Webflow (case-sensitive, with hyphens)

**Nothing publishes:**
→ Using "itemIDs" instead of "itemIds"
→ Collection ID mismatch between Update and Publish

**Verify setup:**
1. Check collection ID matches in Set Fields AND Format for Update
2. Test field names: view Fetch CMS Items output
3. Run with 1 item first before scaling

#### Start Here: Configure Your Collection
Update these values for your Webflow collection:
- **collectionId**: Get from Webflow CMS URL (required)
- **batchSize**: 5-20 items per batch (5 = slower/safer, 20 = faster)
- **Title limits**: 50-60 chars (SEO standard)
- **Summary limits**: 120-155 chars (meta description standard)

Later on, you may increase the values to optimise like url slug, post body, alt-text,etc

#### AI Agent: Customize for Your Fields

**Current setup optimizes:**
- `name` → 50-60 character titles
- `project-summary` → 120-155 character descriptions

**If your collection uses different field names:**
1. Update system prompt (replace "name" and "project-summary", etc)
2. Update Structured Output Parser schema
3. Update Format for Update code (lines 9-10)

**Common Webflow field names:**
- Blog: name, post-summary, post-body
- Products: name, description, short-description
- Portfolio: name, project-summary, project-details

#### Update Collection ID

**Line 23 in this code node MUST match your collection ID.**

**Also update if using different field names:**
- Line 9: `fieldData.name` → your title field
- Line 10: `fieldData['project-summary']` → your description field

#### Summary

All optimizations are logged to Google Sheets. This is optional, remove this node if you don't need it.

#### Fetch CMS Items
Retrieves up to 100 items from your Webflow collection

**Settings:**
- **Endpoint URL**: https://mcp.webflow.com/mcp
- **Tool**: data_cms_tool 
- **Action**: list_collection_items
- **Context**: Brief description of what you're doing (NOT siteId)
- **Authentication**: Remember to set up your credentials first. Instructions are on the yellow sticky note

#### Update Items

Updates fieldData for CMS items

**settings:**
- **Input Mode**: JSON (not parameters)
- **Action**: update_collection_items
- **Timeout**: 60000ms (1 minute for large batches)
- **Authentication**: Remember to set up your credentials first. Instructions are on the yellow sticky note

####  Publish Items

Publishes updated items to live Webflow site

**settings:**
- **Input Mode**: JSON (not parameters)
- **Action**: publish_collection_items
- **Parameter name**: itemIds (case sensitive)
- **Authentication**: Remember to set up your credentials first. Instructions are on the yellow sticky note
